mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and inflation indicates demand-pull price pressures that force monetary tightening, generating negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series rise together, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side contraction that accelerates recession fears, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles produce positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or the predictive power vanishes outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth expands corporate profit margins and revenue forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 320.0 indicate entrenched inflation that erodes real equity valuations and triggers risk-off positioning, yielding negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in over 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above 320.0, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rates signals labor market tightening that boosts consumer spending and drives positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to predict positive 5-day returns, or the joint effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Surging housing starts during a post-shock recovery phase indicate renewed capital investment and economic momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST increases during recovery phases produce negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances, or the signal loses predictive accuracy outside the recovery regime.
